text_blocks.text: meaning "belonging to Warsz", Warsz being a
shortened form of the masculine name of Slavic
origin Warcislaw; see also etymology of Wroclaw.
Folk etymology attributes the city name to a
fisherman, Wars, and his wife, Sawa. According to
legend, Sawa was a mermaid living in the Vistula
River with whom Wars fell in love. In actuality,
Warsz was a 12th/13th-century nobleman who owned
a village located at the modern-day site of
Mariensztat neighbourhood. See also the Vrsovci
family which had escaped to Poland. The official
city name in full is miasto stoleczne Warszawa
(English: "The Capital City of Warsaw"). A native
or resident of Warsaw is known as a Varsovian -
